matches.x: So from the Galilee, the Jordan Valley, the Coastal Plains, or anywhere in the country, it was a journey up to Jerusalem. "When he was twelve years old, they went up to Jerusalem after the custom of the feast." (Luke 2:42.) "The Jews' passover was at hand, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem." (John 2:13.) "When he had thus spoken, he went before, ascending up to Jerusalem." (Luke 19:28.) "He was seen many days of them which came up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem." (Acts 13:31.) "Now when Festus…

Caesarea was on the coast of Palestine, about 70 miles northwest of Jerusalem. Caesarea was at sea level, while the highest point in Jerusalem is 2550 ft. above sea level. “To the Jews, Jerusalem is the high point of temporal and spiritual life. The Holy City is situated in the high hills of Judaea. The New Testament contains the phrase ‘up to Jerusalem’ or ‘up unto Jerusalem’ twenty-two times. Westerners will often view any place north as ‘up north,’ whereas in the Holy Land the region around…

12 Then Festus, when-he had conferred with the council, answered, Hast thouappealed unto Geesar? unto Cesar shalt thou go.

after conferring with the Council, composed of administrative legal advisers, Festus decided that Paul's appeal was a valid one. He turned to the Apostle and said, ‘Hast thou appealed unto Caesar? unto Caesar shalt thou go.’ (Acts 25:12)” (Sidney B. Sperry, Paul's Life and Letters [Salt Lake City: Bookcraft, 1955], 221.)

17 Therefore, when they were o yme hither, without any delay on the lay following I sat on the judgment-seat,

And sat down in the judgment seat Here is another case where John might intentionally used a verb with (kathizein) double meaning. Historically, it would have been unprecedented for Pilate to place Jesus upon the judgment seat. Theologically, Jesus already sat in that position. (Raymond Brown, The Epistles of John, Anchor Bible Series, vol. 29a, p. 881.)

Herod also put the tidal action of the Mediterranean Sea to good use. He constructed the city with a sewer system open to the sea and used the high tides to flush the sewer. This measure certainly cut down on the city's appeal as a seaside swimming resort but was an effective and labor-saving way to keep the city clean. The harbor constructed at Caesarea was, like many of Herod's projects, massive as well as innovative. It was entirely artificial-the first of its kind in the ancient world. It…
